summary: Backend-only testing for PrivyAlgo Blog. 23 pytest tests written covering public endpoints, auth (bearer/cookie), writer RBAC, admin RBAC, uploads, and edge cases. Initial run had 5 failures — most rooted in one real backend bug (ObjectId leak in POST /api/articles response). Fixed that bug + one test assertion; final run 23/23 passing.

critical.issue: Endpoint returned 500 due to ObjectId leak. `insert_one(doc)` mutates the passed dict adding Mongo `_id`, then endpoint returned the same dict → FastAPI serialization crashed with `ObjectId is not iterable`. FIXED by popping `_id` before returning.

minor.issue: slugify strips underscores without replacing them (e.g., 'TEST_Cat_156ffd' -> 'testcat156ffd'). Not breaking, but produces uglier slugs when titles contain underscores. Consider mapping `_` to `-` before stripping.
Endpoint only reads `session_token` from cookie, not from Authorization Bearer header. Clients using bearer auth cannot log out server-side. Consider accepting bearer too.
Writers can self-publish (status='published') directly. Code comment says 'open policy' — confirm this is intentional; otherwise force writer posts to draft and require admin approval.
Returns [] silently when slug not found (200 OK). Consider 404 for consistency with GET /api/articles/{slug}.

action_items: Review the ObjectId fix applied in server.py (POST /api/articles) — same pattern should be audited for any other endpoint that returns a mutated insert_one dict.
Decide on writer publish policy (currently open — writers can publish directly).
Consider improving slugify to preserve underscores as separators.
critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB `_id` leak pattern: any code that does `doc = model.model_dump(); insert_one(doc); return doc` will leak _id. Only /api/articles POST was affected; other endpoints correctly use `find_one({}, {'_id': 0})` or return fresh model dumps.
seed_data() is not idempotent per-item — only runs when the whole collection is empty. Adding a new demo article requires wiping the collection.
`get_current_user` catches expired sessions but doesn't delete them — orphaned sessions accumulate.
No pagination on /api/admin/users (hard cap 500) or /api/my/articles (hard cap 200). OK for now but should be surfaced.
CORS uses `allow_credentials=True` with `allow_origins='*'` fallback — browsers reject this combination. Ensure CORS_ORIGINS is set to explicit origins in production.
`@app.on_event` is deprecated in FastAPI; use lifespan handler.
test_credentials: Seeded ephemeral MongoDB users+sessions (TEST_ prefix) inside pytest fixtures; auto-cleaned on teardown.
seed_data_creation: Two session-scoped fixtures (admin_session, writer_session) inserted test users + user_sessions via mongosh, cleaned up after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. Next agent should focus on frontend UI/integration. Auth flow tested only via manual DB-seeded sessions (real Emergent OAuth not tested — expected). Storage upload works end-to-end (PNG uploaded + downloadable via /api/files/{path}).
rca of the issue: ObjectId leak in POST /api/articles: motor's insert_one() mutates the input dict to add `_id: ObjectId(...)`. Handler stored `doc = art.model_dump()`, awaited insert_one(doc), then `return doc` — but doc now contained an ObjectId. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId → 500. Fix: `doc.pop('_id', None)` before returning (applied). Alternative: re-fetch with `{'_id': 0}` projection.
